title:
Period poverty is real —
and it's happening here.
subtitle: In the UK today, more than one in ten women and people who menstruate struggled to afford period products in the last year. Behind that number are impossible choices no one should have to make.

stats.label: struggled to afford period products for themselves or a dependent in the last year
of those had to prioritise buying food instead
of those wore single-use pads or tampons for longer, risking infection
of those stayed at home as a result — shut out of work, school and daily life

photos.alt: Three friends laughing together — period poverty should never force anyone to sit life out
photos.caption: No one should have to sit life out.

photos.alt: Plastic-free pads on a warm beige background — the products COVERED dispenses free of charge
photos.caption: Plastic-free. Biodegradable. Free.

photos.alt: Students walking through a university campus — the high-footfall venues where COVERED dispensers live
photos.caption: Right where people actually are.
